_description: Target companies for direct ATS board polling. Primary discovery source — preferred over WebSearch because ATS JSON endpoints return structured, fresh, deduplicated data with no 403/JS issues.

_websearch_sources.description: Supplemental discovery sources run via WebSearch each daily pipeline run. Use these to find companies NOT on the ATS watchlist, especially Atlanta startups.

sources.name: Hypepotamus Job Board
sources.disabled_reason: Job listings are JavaScript-rendered via nonce-protected AJAX. Direct WebFetch returns empty shell; WP REST API endpoints for listings are not public. Cannot be polled headlessly without a browser dependency. Tested 2026-05-08.

sources.name: BuiltIn Atlanta
sources.query: site:builtin.com/job ("customer success" OR "technical account" OR "implementation" OR "solutions engineer" OR "support operations" OR "customer operations" OR "deployment") Atlanta
sources.notes: Atlanta tech companies, mid-size focus. Atlanta small company bonus (+20) applies for HQ Atlanta headcount ≤200. Query broadened 2026-06-25 to include customer success + solutions engineer terms.

sources.name: BuiltIn Remote
sources.query: site:builtin.com/job ("customer success" OR "technical account" OR "implementation" OR "solutions engineer" OR "support operations" OR "customer operations" OR "deployment") remote
sources.notes: Remote roles from tech companies nationally. No Atlanta bonus — needs stronger title/keyword fit to clear threshold. Salary floor ($100K) still applies. Query broadened 2026-06-25.

sources.name: Wellfound Startups
sources.query: site:wellfound.com ("customer success" OR "technical account" OR implementation OR "solutions engineer" OR deployment OR "support operations" OR "customer operations") startup remote OR Atlanta
sources.disabled_reason: Tested 2026-06-30: the site:wellfound.com dork returns SEO role-category pages (wellfound.com/role/r/...) and the homepage, not live individual jobs — only ~2 of 10 results were real postings, and stale. Wellfound gates job detail behind auth + client-side rendering (same failure mode as Hypepotamus). Dead weight; it burned a daily WebSearch slot for near-zero yield. Reinvest the slot into the Ashby/Greenhouse/Lever board dorks below, which DO surface live sub-500 boards directly. Revisit only if a non-auth Wellfound listing endpoint appears.

sources.name: AI-Titled Roles (Broad Discovery)
sources.query: ("AI Success Manager" OR "AI Outcomes Manager" OR "AI Adoption" OR "AI Enablement Manager" OR "AI Transformation Lead" OR "AI Value Partner" OR "AI Engagement Lead" OR "AI Delivery Manager" OR "AI GTM") jobs remote 2026
sources.notes: Catch-all WebSearch for novel AI-prefixed role titles that don't match named tiers. Companies are still coining these. Run every pipeline pass. Apply AI/LLM bonus (+20) to any hits. Check for salary floor before surfacing. Score against tier2b_ai_wildcard (+18) if no higher tier matches. ('AI Enablement Manager' added 2026-07-02 after a Crowe posting with that exact Tier-2 title was missed by every discovery source.)
sources.scoring_notes: Apply +20 AI bonus + tier2b_ai_wildcard title match (+18) = strong signal. Verify salary floor.

sources.name: Ashby Boards - Target Roles
sources.query: site:jobs.ashbyhq.com ("customer success" OR "technical account" OR "implementation" OR "enablement" OR "solutions consultant" OR "deployment" OR "professional services" OR "customer engineer") 2026
sources.notes: Discovers Ashby-hosted companies NOT on the watchlist. Returns ~10 results; scan for UNFAMILIAR company names (skip ones already on the watchlist). When a new company appears, verify its board (pipeline/verify_workday.py pattern or a direct Ashby API check) and add it to the watchlist so the poller scans its full roster going forward. Added 2026-06-25. 'enablement' added 2026-07-13 after Superhuman's Partner Technical Enablement Manager (Tier-3 target title class, Remote US, $154K-200K) was invisible to all three board dorks — none carried an enablement term.

sources.name: Greenhouse Boards - Target Roles
sources.query: site:job-boards.greenhouse.io ("technical account manager" OR "customer success" OR "implementation" OR "enablement" OR "solutions engineer" OR "deployment" OR "professional services") 2026
sources.notes: Discovers Greenhouse-hosted companies off the watchlist. Same enroll guidance as Ashby: surface unfamiliar companies, verify board, add to watchlist. Added 2026-06-25. Broadened "customer success manager" -> "customer success" 2026-07-10 after Tailscale's Customer Success Engineer (Greenhouse, exact-phrase Tier-3 target title) was invisible to this query for missing the word-form variant -- same fix as Ashby's query already had. 'enablement' added 2026-07-13 after Superhuman's Partner Technical Enablement Manager (Tier-3 target title class, Remote US, $154K-200K) was invisible to all three board dorks — none carried an enablement term.

sources.name: Lever Boards - Target Roles
sources.query: site:jobs.lever.co ("technical account manager" OR "customer success" OR "implementation" OR "enablement" OR "solutions consultant" OR "deployment") 2026
sources.notes: Discovers Lever-hosted companies off the watchlist. Same enroll guidance. Added 2026-06-25. Broadened "customer success manager" -> "customer success" 2026-07-10, same fix as the Greenhouse query (Tailscale miss). 'enablement' added 2026-07-13 after Superhuman's Partner Technical Enablement Manager (Tier-3 target title class, Remote US, $154K-200K) was invisible to all three board dorks — none carried an enablement term.

sources.name: AI-Native & AI-Safety Orgs (vertical discovery)
sources.query: ("AI safety" OR "AI security" OR "AI governance" OR "frontier model" OR "applied AI" OR "AI research" OR "contact center AI" OR "customer support AI") ("customer success" OR "technical account" OR operations OR implementation OR deployment OR solutions) jobs 2026
sources.notes: Catches AI-native, AI-safety, AI-security, and AI-governance orgs that are off the watchlist and whose fitting roles may be titled differently (e.g. 'Operations Generalist'). Vertical/company discovery, not title-keyed — surface the COMPANY, then verify board + add to watchlist so the poller scans its full roster. Apply AI bonus (+20) to in-vertical hits. Added 2026-06-25 after a manually-found FAR.AI role exposed that the discovery layer was too title-narrow; 'AI security'/'AI governance'/'technical account' added 2026-07-02 after WitnessAI (AI security, 102 people, Atlanta-hybrid TAM) was invisible to this query for the same reason.

sources.name: 80,000 Hours Job Board (EA/AI-safety/public-good orgs)
sources.query: site:jobs.80000hours.org ("Operations" OR "Program Manager" OR "Customer Success" OR "Technical Program Manager" OR "Implementation" OR "Business Operations")
sources.notes: ACCESS UPGRADED 2026-07-13: the board is directly queryable via its public Algolia backend -- no browser, no JS rendering, no WebSearch snippets needed. POST to https://W6KM1UDIB3-dsn.algolia.net/1/indexes/jobs_prod/query with headers x-algolia-application-id: W6KM1UDIB3 and x-algolia-api-key: d1d7f2c8696e7b36837d5ed337c4a319 (public search key embedded in the site frontend), body {"query": "<terms>", "hitsPerPage": N}. Hits carry title, company_name, salary, tags_city/tags_country, description (HTML), url_external (apply link), posted_at, closes_at, experience_min/avg, company_url. Query the target-role terms (Operations, Program Manager, Customer Success, Implementation, Business Operations) rather than pulling the whole index. This retires the lossy WebSearch site: dork (confirmed undercount 2026-07-06: ~5 org pages surfaced vs ~84+ live listings) and invalidates the 2026-07-06 'needs a browser tool, not worth it' premise -- same Algolia pattern as harvest_hn_hiring.py. The old WebSearch query below remains as fallback only. Org pages at jobs.80000hours.org/organisations/{slug} still render server-side for per-org verification. Caveats unchanged: research/engineering titles dominate; some roles volunteer/unpaid or Director/Chair-level -- salary floor and seniority filters still apply. Found 2026-07-13 while assessing a user-surfaced Frame Fellowship Operations Lead role the WebSearch dork had never surfaced.
sources.disabled_reason: Superseded 2026-07-13 by pipeline/poll_80k.py, which queries the board's public Algolia backend directly (see api_access below and the feeder's docstring). The WebSearch site: dork was a lossy proxy (~5 org pages per run vs 84+ live listings, confirmed 2026-07-06). Re-enable only if the Algolia key rotates and the feeder breaks; the org pages at jobs.80000hours.org/organisations/{slug} remain WebFetch-able for per-org verification either way.

sources.name: Workday Boards - Target Roles
sources.query: site:myworkdayjobs.com ("AI Enablement" OR "technical account manager" OR "customer success manager" OR "support operations" OR "implementation manager") Atlanta OR remote
sources.notes: Closes the Workday blind spot found 2026-07-02: a Crowe 'AI Enablement Manager' (Atlanta, Tier-2 title, salary in range) was invisible to the pipeline because board dorks only covered Ashby/Greenhouse/Lever and Crowe isn't watchlist material. Workday hosts the enterprise segment specifically. Individual myworkdayjobs.com pages are JS-rendered — do NOT WebFetch them; verify hits via the Workday CXS API instead (POST https://{host}/wday/cxs/{tenant}/{site}/jobs with searchText — see _workday_notes / verify_workday.py). Enroll recurring-fit companies onto the watchlist with wd_host/wd_tenant/wd_site.

sources.name: Passion Verticals (EV/Health/Agtech discovery)
sources.query: ("EV charging" OR "electric vehicle" OR "climate tech" OR "digital health" OR healthtech OR agtech OR "vertical farming") ("customer success manager" OR "technical account manager" OR "implementation manager" OR "support operations" OR "solutions engineer") jobs remote OR Atlanta
sources.notes: Vertical discovery for Aneesh's passion domains (see _scoring_config → passion_domains). Hits get the +10 passion bonus (under the +30 company cap). Unfamiliar companies with a Greenhouse/Ashby/Lever board go to the enrollment queue like any other discovery hit; passion-domain companies get the same sub-500 enrollment bias.

sources.name: Industrial IoT / Physical-World Hardware (discovery only, no scoring bonus)
sources.query: (site:jobs.ashbyhq.com OR site:job-boards.greenhouse.io OR site:jobs.lever.co) ("industrial IoT" OR "condition monitoring" OR "predictive maintenance" OR "industrial automation" OR "asset tracking") ("customer success" OR "technical account" OR "solutions engineer" OR "implementation" OR deployment)
sources.notes: Added 2026-07-18 after Aneesh described being drawn to physical-world/industrial-equipment sensing (anchor example: a sensor device on spinning machinery) — narrower than generic 'hardware' or 'wireless communication' on purpose, to avoid pulling in consumer telecom/carrier noise or double-counting his existing consumer-IoT background (First Alert/Newell/Luma), which already scores normally via keyword overlap. A plain (non-site-scoped) version of this query was tested first and returned mostly generic aggregator category pages (ZipRecruiter/Indeed/SimplyHired), not enrollable individual companies -- switched to the ATS-host-scoped pattern (same shape as the Ashby/Greenhouse/Lever 'Boards - Target Roles' sources) since that's what actually surfaces live individual postings; first live run found AssetWatch (industrial condition-monitoring platform), Advanced Technology Services, and ANYbotics (industrial robotics/predictive maintenance). UNLIKE the Passion Verticals source above, this is discovery/enrollment-only: hits get NO passion-domain scoring bonus (Aneesh explicitly deferred that decision -- see memory user_profile.md 'Open item, deliberately deferred'). Companies surfaced here go through the normal enrollment verification and are scored on the standard rubric like any other company -- no bonus, no special treatment, just a wider discovery net. Do not add a passion_domains entry for this vertical unless Aneesh explicitly asks.

sources.name: Tier-1 Manager-Title Sweep (all 3 ATSes at once)
sources.query: (site:jobs.lever.co OR site:job-boards.greenhouse.io OR site:jobs.ashbyhq.com) ("Technical Support Operations Manager" OR "Support Operations Manager" OR "Customer Operations Manager" OR "Technical Support Manager" OR "Customer Support Manager" OR "Technical Operations Manager" OR "Support Engineering Manager")
sources.notes: Added 2026-07-14 after a test run: a rare-exact-title sweep across all three major ATSes at once surfaces companies the broader 'customer success/technical account/implementation' dorks miss (those get crowded out by large, well-known companies). Found Model N (Manager, Support Operations) and Aledade (Director, Application Support Operations — health-tech passion domain) on the first run. Deliberately scoped to Manager-level Tier-1 titles only — 'Head of Support' and 'Director of Support Operations' excluded per Aneesh: those read as above his current level/purview, not a fit worth surfacing even though they're still scored as tier1_true_match in _title_scoring_tiers for ATS-poller hits (open question whether that config should change too — see _title_scoring_tiers note).

sources.name: SmartRecruiters Boards - Target Roles
sources.query: site:jobs.smartrecruiters.com ("customer success" OR "technical account manager" OR "implementation" OR "support operations")
sources.notes: Added 2026-07-14 after a test run found this ATS type had zero discovery coverage despite poll_ats.py supporting it (see _smartrecruiters_notes). Surfaced Nexthink (multiple live CSM/Senior CSM reqs across US cities) on the first run. Same enroll guidance as the Ashby/Greenhouse/Lever dorks: surface unfamiliar companies, verify the board via the SmartRecruiters postings API, add to watchlist.

_smartrecruiters_notes: ats='smartrecruiters'. slug is the case-sensitive company identifier (e.g. 'BoschGroup', not 'bosch') — find it from a careers.smartrecruiters.com/{slug} or jobs.smartrecruiters.com/{slug} URL. Public postings API, no auth. Poller fetches one page (limit=100); fine for small companies. Added 2026-06-30 to widen enrollable boards beyond GH/Ashby/Lever/Workday.
_workday_notes: Workday entries use ats='workday' and carry wd_host (incl. wdN datacenter), wd_tenant, and wd_site instead of relying on slug for the endpoint (slug is still set = tenant, for dedup-key prefixing). The public careers page is JS-rendered; poll_ats.py POSTs to the internal CXS JSON API instead. Salary is NOT in the list response (it's on each job's detail page), so the poller treats it as neutral and Claude pulls exact salary + posting date from the JD at tailoring time, where the salary-floor and 21-day freshness gates are applied. wd_host/wd_tenant/wd_site are NOT guessable per company - verify new tenants with pipeline/verify_workday.py before adding (find the exact careers URL first, e.g. via 'site:myworkdayjobs.com [company]').

_scoring_config.notes_near_miss: Near-misses are listed (with links) in the email digest but NOT tailored, so Aneesh can eyeball them. Two kinds, unioned: (A) score near-miss — passes all hard filters incl. salary, but scores below light_tailoring_threshold (no lower score bound, capture all). (B) salary near-miss — passes all other filters but salary midpoint is below salary_floor_usd yet at or above near_miss_salary_floor_usd. See SKILL Step 2 + Step 5.
_scoring_config.notes_salary: Compare the MIDPOINT of a listed base-salary range against salary_floor_usd — never the bottom or top. Single figure: use as-is. OTE-only: estimate base (80% for variable/sales roles, 100% otherwise). No salary listed: do not filter, treat as neutral in scoring. See SKILL Step 2b for the authoritative rule.
_scoring_config.notes_freshness: Add +10 for postings ≤2 days old, +2 for ≤7 days. Validated by empirical data: response rates fall sharply after 48 hours.
_scoring_config.notes_small_company: Counterweight for the sub-500 segment, which the watchlist + AI/Atlanta bonuses structurally bury (those bonuses correlate with big, well-known companies). Add +15 if the company's headcount_band is ≤50-200, +8 if 201-500, 0 above. Applies ONLY when the company entry carries a headcount_band field; if absent, treat as neutral (0) — do NOT guess. This is a COMPANY-LEVEL bonus and counts toward the +30 company-bonus cap in CLAUDE.md alongside AI/ML, watchlist, Atlanta, IoT. Consequence by design: a small AI-native company (AI +20 + small +15 = 35 → clamped to 30) gains little since it already surfaces; the lift lands where it's needed — small NON-AI companies (vertical SaaS, dev infra) like PermitFlow/Antithesis/Mintlify that have real role fit but no AI/Atlanta bonus to clear threshold. Rationale: at a 150-person company a support-ops/CSM/implementation hire has real scope; at a 3,000-person company it's one of dozens.
_scoring_config.notes: Light tailoring for IC-level roles 78-87. Full tailoring for 88+. Skip below 78. Capped companies require 110+ regardless of tier. Cap rule: count entries where applied=true AND outcome=null (pending applications). If that count ≥ company_cap_max_applied_pending, new roles need company_cap_threshold to surface. Queued/unapplied roles do NOT count toward the cap — only submitted applications do.

passion_domains.notes: Mission-alignment bonus for Aneesh's personal interest areas (added 2026-07-02, from direct user input). Apply +10 ONCE per job if the company's product/mission sits in ANY of these domains; do not stack across domains. This is a COMPANY-LEVEL bonus and counts toward the +30 company-bonus cap alongside AI/ML, watchlist, Atlanta, IoT, small-company. Claude applies it SEMANTICALLY at Step 2c (company mission/product, not keyword accidents like 'patient rollout'). The poller uses poller_keywords only for a small pre_score lift so passion companies reach the shortlist.

electrification_ev.description: EVs, charging, batteries, energy storage, grid, clean energy, climate tech

health_tech.description: Digital health, health devices, patient-facing platforms, healthcare interoperability

agriculture_gardening_food.description: Agtech, farming, gardening, horticulture, food systems (Aneesh heads a community garden steering committee)

_title_scoring_tiers.description: Aneesh's background is Technical Support Operations Manager — he runs a support function end-to-end: hiring/training agents, AI deployment, knowledge base, Salesforce administration, QA auditing, BPO management. Score title match accordingly. Operations-first and implementation-first roles are true matches; pure relationship CSM is a stretch.

tier3_reasonable_stretch.notes: CSM roles are structurally different (account/relationship ownership vs. operations leadership) but viable when the JD emphasizes technical depth, deployment, or team management.

tier4_weak_stretch.notes: Only worth surfacing if AI bonus, Atlanta bonus, or salary pushes score above 78 threshold.
tier4_weak_stretch.forward_deployed_engineer_note: Demoted from Tier 2 (+22) to Tier 4 (+8) on 2026-07-09 after a near-100% miss rate across 7+ runs (2026-06-25 through 2026-07-09) covering dozens of postings at Parloa, Modal, Baseten, Antithesis, PermitFlow, Roboflow, Komodo Health, and Confido. In the 2026 AI-infra hiring market this title has drifted to mean 'customer-facing software engineer who ships production code' (Kubernetes, Terraform, sometimes a CS-degree requirement), not deployment strategy or ops — a real skill mismatch with the master resume, not a scoring artifact. ALWAYS read the actual JD before tailoring an FDE role, regardless of score — do not trust title-tier alone for this specific title. Titles that tested well and stayed at Tier 2: Deployment Strategist, AI Deployment Manager, AI Deployment Lead.

tier2b_ai_wildcard.description: Catch-all for novel AI-prefixed roles companies are coining in 2025-2026. Apply when a title is NOT matched by tier1 or tier2 BUT satisfies: (1) 'AI' appears as a word-boundary token, AND (2) title contains ANY of: customer, deployment, implementation, engagement, outcomes, adoption, success, transformation, strategy, value, delivery, operations, solutions. Score as +18 (between T2 +22 and T3 +15). Flag for manual review in email digest. Do NOT apply to 'AI Engineer' or 'AI Architect' titles (those are IC/coding roles).

_poller_config.description: Runtime matching knobs for pipeline/poll_ats.py; the poller reads this block plus _title_scoring_tiers at startup and carries NO hardcoded title lists of its own. Exact-title gate = every title in _title_scoring_tiers (all tiers, plus tier2b explicit_titles) + supplemental_exact_titles below. Matching is stemmed-token-subset: a job title matches a config title when it contains all of the config title's word stems, in any order and any word form ('Manager, Technical Account Management' matches 'Technical Account Manager'; 'Support Ops Manager' matches 'Support Operations Manager'). To teach the poller a new title concept, add it here or to a scoring tier; never edit poll_ats.py.

supplemental_exact_titles.notes: Titles the poller surfaces for review that don't carry a scoring tier in _title_scoring_tiers. They pre-score at title_match_prescore. If one becomes a recurring real fit, promote it to a tier instead of leaving it here.

borderline_fragments.notes: A title matching >= min_fragments of these (stemmed-token match) lands in the poller's borderline list for Claude's semantic review. Single words match as whole-word stems, so 'ai' no longer needs a trailing-space hack and now matches titles that END in 'AI' too.

function_mismatch_titles.notes: Added 2026-07-19 after these title classes consumed ~13 of 25 shortlist slots and were manually skipped as 'poor function fit' in every run since at least 07-14 (Maven AGI PMs held the day's top two pre-scores). Titles matching these patterns are DEMOTED out of the shortlist into the output's function_mismatch section (kept as digest FYI lines, never scored or tailored). Protected tiers override the demotion: a title whose best tier match is tier1/tier2/tier4 is never demoted, so 'Product Engagement Manager, User Operations' (tier1, contains 'Product…Manager'), 'Support Engineering Manager' (tier1, contains 'Engineering Manager'), and 'Product Customer Success Manager' (tier4, contains 'Product…Manager') stay live. tier3/supplemental/tier2b/borderline matches get checked. 'Product Manager', 'Technical Program Manager', and 'Sales Engineer' were moved here FROM supplemental_exact_titles, where their 'surface for review' intent had turned into daily shortlist crowding. If one of these classes ever produces a real fit, move that specific title variant to a scoring tier.

jd_verification_required_titles.notes: Titles with a documented history of JD-level mismatch despite matching the gate (see tier4 forward_deployed_engineer_note). The poller tags matches jd_verification_required=true, and when filling the per-company diversity cap it takes a company's NON-flagged roles first, so a risky top pick can't crowd out a safer second-best title from the same company (the Confido 2026-07-09 failure mode). Flagged roles still surface when slots remain. ALWAYS read the full JD before tailoring one of these, regardless of score. 'AI Enablement Program Manager' added 2026-07-15 after Klaviyo's posting under that exact title (tier2_strong_overlap, pre_score 53, looked like a strong fit) turned out to be an Engineering-department developer-experience/onboarding role requiring 5-8+ years of software engineering and Git/CI/CD proficiency -- zero overlap with support/CS-flavored AI enablement work. The title alone is not a reliable signal for this company category; some orgs use 'AI Enablement' for internal engineering tooling, not customer/adoption-facing AI rollout.

companies.name: EvolutionIQ
companies.bonus_reason: AI/ML platform (+20) — AI guidance platform for disability/injury claims management
companies.reason: AI claims-guidance platform (NYC, ~242 employees, acquired by CCC Intelligent Solutions Dec 2024 for $730M, still hires on its own Greenhouse board). Verified live board 2026-07-19: Agentic Solutions Consultant (NY/Chicago), Solutions Engineer (NY). The 'Technical Engagement Manager / Account Manager (AI/SaaS)' that surfaced it via the dork had already closed by verification time.

companies.name: ServiceNow
companies.reason: Acquired Moveworks (AI for IT service management) — Moveworks roles now post on ServiceNow's SmartRecruiters board with a '- Moveworks' title suffix, e.g. 'Senior Customer Success Manager - Moveworks' (NY/Tokyo/London seen 2026-07-15). This is why Moveworks' own Greenhouse board has 404'd since 2026-06-18 — it didn't move ATS, it got absorbed. NOTE: board has 416 total postings and poll_ats.py's SmartRecruiters fetch is single-page (limit=100, see _smartrecruiters_notes) — deep/older Moveworks-branded postings past the first page of (mostly unrelated, large-corp) results may not surface. Title matching still filters noise from the page it does see.
